body.title: CLOSED: Sprint Step N — teach-Igor palace deposit step
body.result: Added Step 7.5 to /sprint and Step 6.5 to /sprint-batch per-ticket loop: 'what would I deposit into Igor's palace from this work?' Default-skip is load-bearing; deposit only when novel reasoning emerged (non-obvious WHY, hidden invariant, workaround mechanism, root-vs-symptom). Synced to agent_datacenter master + pushed (2334236).
body.description:
**Approved 2026-05-03 (Akien — 'all is approved! go!').** Workflow consumer ticket: closes the lapsed practice 'teach Igor as you implement tickets'. Gap diagnosis: skills don't ask, so CC ships code without depositing the reasoning into Igor's palace.

Add Step N (post-test, pre-commit) to /sprint and /sprint-batch: 'what would you deposit into Igor's palace from this work?' Default answer: skip if no novel reasoning. When non-skip: propose 0-N palace nodes (path + title + content), Akien-review inline, then commit via memory_palace INSERT or memory_get/set MCP tool.

Default-skip is load-bearing — most sprint work is mechanical and shouldn't pollute the palace. The step exists to catch the cases where novel design reasoning emerges and would otherwise be lost.

**Affected files:** /home/akien/.claude/skills/sprint/SKILL.md (insert Step N before commit), /home/akien/.claude/skills/sprint-batch/SKILL.md (same — per-ticket loop)
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/capability-protocol (parent — workflow consumer), theigors/rules/memory (verify, delegate, persist), theigors/rules/docs-live-in-code (reasoning that lives in palace ≠ reasoning that lives in code).
**Scope boundary:** IN: Step N docs in both sprint skills, default-skip framing, examples of when-to-deposit (novel reasoning, design choice with non-obvious why, refactor that surfaces a hidden invariant). OUT: enforcing deposits (CC still decides). OUT: automatic palace-node draft generation (manual proposal for now).
**Test plan:** CC.1 integration test loading /sprint with a fixture ticket — assert Step N prompts the executor and accepts a 'skip' default. Manual: complete a real sprint and verify the step fires (and that 'skip' is the easy/default path).
